analysis_result: WNT_UP.V1_UP in MPNST-G1 (vs MPNST-G2): NES = -0.905, FDR q-val = 0.671 (leading edge: 43/128 genes).
denominator_used: N=12 MPNST tumors with RNA-Seq available: 6 MPNST-G1 (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803) vs 6 MPNST-G2 (M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413). M1933 (G2) is listed in Clinical Data as having RNA-Seq but is NOT present in GSE207400_Matrix columns, so it was excluded. Ranked gene list n=14,992 genes after collapsing duplicates.
assumptions_made: Ranking metric = Welch t-statistic (equal_var=False) computed per gene across samples with G1 as phenotype of interest (positive stat = up in G1, negative = up in G2); duplicate gene symbols collapsed by keeping the row with maximum |t|; MSigDB C6 gene set 'WNT_UP.V1_UP' was obtained from gseapy library 'MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures' (Enrichr key 'WNT UP.V1 UP'); gseapy.prerank used with 1000 permutations, seed=42, min_size=5, max_size=5000; G2 sample M1933 excluded because absent from GSE207400 expression matrix; GSE207400 matrix values assumed to be log-normalised expression as stated in data context.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical: /data/41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data' (108 rows x 15 cols). Relevant cols: Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq.
- Expression: /data/GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz (14,995 genes x 48 samples, log-normalized; 0 NaN).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Filter 1: RNA-Seq == 'X' and Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2} -> 6 G1 + 7 G2 = 13 Tumor_IDs.
- Filter 2: Tumor_ID present in expression matrix columns -> drops M1933 (G2) which has RNA-Seq in the clinical sheet but is not a column in GSE207400_Matrix -> 6 G1 + 6 G2 = 12 MPNST samples.
- Final analytic cohort: 12 samples (6 MPNST-G1, 6 MPNST-G2).

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Phenotype of interest: MPNST_G1 (positive sign for ranking stat).
- G1 = {M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803}. G2 = {M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413}.
- Gene set: WNT_UP.V1_UP (MSigDB C6 oncogenic signatures). Retrieved via gseapy.get_library('MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures','Human') under Enrichr key 'WNT UP.V1 UP' (180 genes; 128 overlap with the ranked expression list).

D. COMPUTATION
- Per-gene ranking stat = Welch t-statistic (G1 vs G2) with nan_policy='omit'; duplicate gene symbols collapsed by max |t|; final ranked list = 14,992 genes sorted descending.
- gseapy.prerank(rnk, gene_sets={'WNT_UP.V1_UP':<180 genes>}, permutation_num=1000, seed=42, threads=4, min_size=5, max_size=5000, outdir=None).

E. RESULT
- ES = -0.2108
- NES = -0.905
- NOM p-val = 0.671
- FDR q-val = 0.671
- FWER p-val = 0.671
- Tag% = 43/128 (leading edge genes)
- Direction: negative NES indicates that WNT_UP.V1_UP genes tend to be *lower* in MPNST-G1 relative to MPNST-G2 (i.e., not enriched in G1) at this ranking.

F. CAVEATS
- Small sample sizes (6 vs 6) produce noisy Welch t-statistics; rank stability affects NES/FDR reliability.
- Only one gene set tested, so FDR q-val equals nominal p-val (gseapy BH correction across the supplied set).
- Sample M1933 (G2) is flagged as RNA-Seq='X' in the clinical sheet but missing from GSE207400_Matrix columns and was therefore excluded.
- gseapy Enrichr-flavored 'MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures' gene sets correspond to MSigDB C6 v-family signatures; gene membership is expected to match WNT_UP.V1_UP (180 genes).
- Paper's Supplementary Fig 6a used fgsea (per Methods); absolute NES and FDR values may differ because of (i) ranking metric choice (paper may use signal-to-noise or limma moderated-t), (ii) permutation backend, and (iii) duplicate handling. The sign interpretation here (G1 = phenotype of interest) is as requested.
